story: Swords clashed and ships sailed as Spain explored the Americas. But in 1503, Queen Isabella took a bold step. title: All Aboard Russia's First Public Railway!
story: Choo-choo! The Russian Empire's first public railway chugged to life in 1837. On October 30, the Tsarskoye Selo Railway began its journey, connecting Saint Petersburg to Pavlovsk. This groundbreaking line stretched for many kilometers, paving the way for a new era of transportation in Russia. Passengers marveled at the speed and convenience of this iron horse.
activity_question: What was the name of Russia's first public railway?
bonus_fact: The word 'tsarskoye' means 'tsar's,' so this railway was named after the tsar's village!
